mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 8.0 percent, labor market slack dissipates rapidly, triggering wage growth expectations and boosting consumer spending, which lifts equity prices within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 8.0, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and retail sales indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion, which drives corporate revenue growth and sustains positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over any 12-month rolling period, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, industrial production growth decelerates while capacity utilization peaks, leading to margin compression and earnings downgrades that drive negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth remains positive while S&P 500 forward returns stay positive for more than 70% of observations, invalidating the margin compression thesis.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month cumulative increase in the CPI exceeding 1.5 percent signals entrenched inflation, forcing the Federal Reserve to tighten policy aggressively, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 60% of quarters following a CPI spike above the threshold, or real yields fail to rise in response to the inflation reading.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid nonfarm payroll growth combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market recovery, which accelerates consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces a negative predictive rank correlation over any 3-year window, or payroll growth decouples from unemployment declines without corresponding equity outperformance.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP exceeding 2.0 percent annualized rate reflects robust aggregate demand, which supports corporate earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth above 2.0 percent consistently coincides with negative or flat 20-day equity returns, or the signal's information coefficient drops below zero over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds 98.0 while housing starts fall below 700, supply chain bottlenecks collide with weakening demand, causing inventory gluts and earnings misses that drive negative S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or inventory-to-sales ratios do not rise during the specified divergence period.
